profile.name: Mary
profile.role_in_household: Primary earner and self-employed business owner; tends to drive planning priorities and meeting agendas
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable discussing accounts, taxes, and tradeoffs, but prefers clear summaries over deep technical detail
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Growth-seeking and action-oriented; willing to tolerate volatility if the plan feels intentional and progress is measurable, but gets impatient with overly conservative recommendations
profile.communication_style: Direct and fast-paced; uses businesslike phrasing, occasionally interrupts to keep things moving; will say things like “roughly” or “ballpark” when recalling details and asks for the bottom line
emotional_tendencies: anxious about losing momentum or missing opportunities
profile.decision_making_style: Prefers a clear recommendation with 2–3 options; decides quickly once she understands the rationale and the next steps
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open: will trust faster if the advisor is organized, follows through, and respects her time
internal_biases: overconfidence in ability to “figure it out later”
recency bias (recent market moves influence urgency)
hidden_concerns: worries about how her fair health could affect long-term plans without wanting to dwell on it
concerned about keeping flexibility while still funding long-range goals

profile.name: Michae
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; retired and more focused on stability and cash flow, often acting as the “numbers checker” in discussions
profile.financial_literacy_level: High: detail-oriented and comfortable reconciling figures; tends to ask clarifying questions and correct small inaccuracies
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: More cautious in day-to-day behavior despite the household’s aggressive posture; prefers guardrails (cash buffer, clear downside plan) before agreeing to higher-risk moves
profile.communication_style: Measured and precise; pauses to think, asks follow-up questions, and may gently correct terms or amounts; uses fewer hedges but will say “I’m not sure” rather than guess
emotional_tendencies: reassured by written summaries and clear tracking
profile.decision_making_style: Deliberate and verification-driven; prefers to review numbers and implications before committing, and likes decisions documented
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Earned trust: starts neutral-to-skeptical and warms up when the advisor demonstrates accuracy and transparency
internal_biases: loss aversion (focuses on what could go wrong first)
anchoring to specific figures once stated
hidden_concerns: concerned about relying on multiple income streams and wants clarity on what is dependable versus variable
worries about being “behind” relative to retirement timelines but doesn’t want to alarm Mary
